intro: Hakea lanata, also known as the woolly hakea, is a flowering plant species native to Australia, characterized by its silvery-gray, woolly foliage and white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Hakea lanata include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hakea lanata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.
